title: Pan Seared Punjabi Wonton Dumplings
description: A briny and crisp nostalgic Indian dish featuring wonton skin, this recipe is a dairy-free friendly version of traditional Punjabi dumplings, cooked to perfection in a terracotta dish.

ingredients.name: wonton skin
ingredients.notes: round or square skin can be used

instructions: Preheat the terracotta dish over medium heat on the stovetop.
Add oil to the preheated dish and swirl it around to coat the bottom.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to brown.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Add the mashed potato, peas, garam masala, and salt, and stir well to combine.
Lay a wonton skin on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the potato mixture in the center of the skin.
Dip your finger in a small amount of water and run it along the edges of the wonton skin.
Fold the skin into a triangle by bringing the two opposite corners together to form a point, and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Repeat the process with the remaining wonton skin and filling.
Add the dumplings to the terracotta dish, leaving some space between each dumpling.
Pan sear the dumplings for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Serve the dumplings hot with your favorite chutney or raita.
